---
title: "Analyzing US Births and Basketball Recruits"
author: "Suhani Shah"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---


```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
# Read the Excel file from your data folder
my_data <- read_excel("data/us_births_1994_2014.xlsx")

# View the structure of the data
glimpse(my_data)
library(readxl)
df <- read_excel("data/us_births_1994_2014.xlsx")
summary(df[c("births", "year")])

```

```{r}
#| cache: true
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)

births_tibble <- read_excel("data/us_births_1994_2014.xlsx") %>%
  mutate(day_of_week = factor(day_of_week,
    levels = c("Sun", "Mon", "Tues", "Wed", "Thurs", "Fri", "Sat"),
    ordered = TRUE))

births_tibble |>
  group_by(month, date_of_month) |>
  summarize(mean_births = mean(births), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = date_of_month, y = fct_rev(factor(month)), fill = mean_births)) +
  geom_tile()
  births_tibble |>
  filter(month == 12) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarize(
    christmas_births = births[date_of_month == 25],
    baseline_births = mean(births[date_of_month %in% c(20:24, 27:30)]),
    percentage = (christmas_births / baseline_births) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```
```{r}
christmas_data <- births_tibble |>
  filter(month == 12) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarize(
    christmas_births = births[date_of_month == 25],
    baseline_births = mean(births[date_of_month %in% c(20:24, 27:30)]),
    percentage = (christmas_births / baseline_births) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
summary(christmas_data)
```

The `pct_of_baseline` ranges from roughly 48% to 75% — a 27-point swing across just 21 Christmases, with a


```{r}
christmas_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = percentage)) +  # Changed here
  geom_line(color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.5) +

  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Dec 25 Births as a Share of Surrounding-Day Baseline",
    subtitle = "Year-over-year comparison looks noisy",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Dec 25 births as % of baseline",
    caption = "Source: FiveThirtyEight/SSA (1994–2014)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
  births_model <- lm(births ~ factor(year) + factor(month) + day_of_week, data = births_tibble)
summary(births_model)$r.squared

```


```{r}
births_adjusted <- births_tibble %>%
  mutate(pct_resid = resid(births_model) / mean(births) * 100)
calendar_resid <- births_adjusted %>%
  # Filter out Leap Day
  filter(!(month == 2 & date_of_month == 29)) %>%
  # Group by month and day
  group_by(month, date_of_month) %>%
  # Compute the mean residual for each of the 365 days
  summarize(mean_resid = mean(pct_resid, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  # Assign a calendar date using 2001 as the anchor year
  mutate(date = make_date(year = 2001, month = month, day = date_of_month))

calendar_resid

```

```{r}
calendar_resid |>
  ggplot(aes(x = calendar_date, y = mean_pct_resid)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray60") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2001-01-01"), y = -38, label = "Jan. 1",  size = 3, hjust = 0.2) +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2001-07-04"), y = -14, label = "July 4",  size = 3, hjust = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2001-12-25"), y = -38, label = "Dec. 25", size = 3, hjust = 1) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b.", date_breaks = "3 months") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Fewer Babies Are Born on Holidays",
    subtitle = "US births relative to year-, month-, and weekday-adjusted baseline (1994–2014)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "% vs. adjusted baseline",
    caption = "Source: FiveThirtyEight/SSA (1994–2014)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
